posts.author: janadel (Yiannis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

  1. Is there any way to make sure that all users of a board have the same default sorting when they enter a board ?
  2. Can I restrict users to save their own sorting ?
  3. Can I use the API to get the sorting settings for a user, board combination ?

Thanks

posts.author: FredBaker (Integrated Human | Authorized Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @janadel !

Yiannis:

Is there any way to make sure that all users of a board have the same default sorting when they enter a board ?

When you sort a board, you can save the sort as a new view using the “Save as new view” button in the upper right corner of the sort dialog (found in the secondary menu above the top group on the board)

Yiannis:

Can I restrict users to save their own sorting ?

You can control board permissions for users using the board permissions option from the 3 dot menu in the upper right side next to the Invite box. There are board permissions to restrict editing permissions from editing anything down to only writing updates and not being able to edit. There is also an option so users can only edit items they are assigned to in a people column.

You will need to confirm yourself that this prevents them from changing views, as I didn’t test this (I don’t have other users in my monday.com instance right now).

Screenshot 2023-06-16 134531918×384 23.4 KB

Yiannis:

Can I use the API to get the sorting settings for a user, board combination ?

This I don’t know about, and will leave to others to answer.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: janadel (Yiannis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for responding.
Unfortunately, i only have “Save to this View” and i can not see “Save as new View”

Screenshot 2023-06-17 0918171392×726 46.3 KB

posts.author: FredBaker (Integrated Human | Authorized Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @janadel,

It looks like you are already in a created view, so the dialog is asking to update the current view, rather than creating a new view. In the views tabs across the top, would you make sure you are in the main table view all the way on the left? Then try again and see if that provides the new view.

Alternatively (working from memory as I’m on my phone) there should be a plus or new view button to the right of the views you have established. You can create a new view there and save it as default.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @FredBaker for the answers!

Regarding the API question, @janadel as of today there is no way to get the items sorting of a board view via API.

Let us know if you have any other questions!

Cheers,
Matias
